target.title: Draft: security: add Security Matrix runtime-fact audit model
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds an inert Security Matrix runtime-fact evaluator.
- Models actor, influence source, tool capability, approval state, and operator policy as separate inputs.
- Adds default policy data, a deterministic evaluator, tool capability resolution, an audit-event object builder, tests, and documentation.

## Scope

This PR is model-only and audit-only.

It does not block tool calls, require confirmation at runtime, emit runtime diagnostic events, add config, change UI, change plugin behavior, or alter runtime tool execution.

## Validation

Validated locally against `upstream/main`.

- `upstream/main`: `3d6252a5177259cf7afa9602cfbe7e17350e8166`
- PR head: `3bd417ec953baeee239c978e091822ffb96799f4`

Commands run:

```bash
pnpm test src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts
pnpm changed:lanes --base upstream/main --json
pnpm exec oxlint src/security/security-matrix/
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/security/security-matrix.md
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
pnpm docs:list | grep -n "security/security-matrix.md"
```

Results:

- 25 focused tests passed
- changed-lanes reported `core=true`, `coreTests=true`, `docs=true`, `tooling=true`
- oxlint reported 0 warnings and 0 errors
- docs formatting reported clean
- git whitespace check reported clean
- docs index included `security/security-matrix.md`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: The Security Matrix evaluator classifies runtime tool facts using actor, influence source, capability, approval state, and operator policy. Actor identity is modeled separately from content influence, and policy decisions are produced from the runtime fact set.

Real environment tested: Local Linux checkout at `/home/rev/projects/openclaw`, branch `security/matrix-audit-model`, with `upstream/main` at `3d6252a5177259cf7afa9602cfbe7e17350e8166` and PR head at `3bd417ec953baeee239c978e091822ffb96799f4`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: From `~/projects/openclaw`, ran `pnpm test src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts`, `pnpm changed:lanes --base upstream/main --json`, `pnpm exec oxlint src/security/security-matrix/`, `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/security/security-matrix.md`, `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`, and `pnpm docs:list | grep -n "security/security-matrix.md"`.

Evidence: Terminal output from the local checkout showed `unit-fast src/security/security-matrix/evaluate.test.ts (25 tests)`, `Test Files 1 passed`, `Tests 25 passed`, `node scripts/changed-lanes.mjs --base upstream/main --json` listing the 8 expected paths, `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors`, `Docs formatting clean (663 files)`, no output from `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`, and `1152:security/security-matrix.md - Inert audit model for source-aware Security Matrix decisions` from the docs index command.

Observed result: The evaluator tests passed and covered the expected policy matrix behavior, including externally influenced restricted classes, externally influenced state-changing classes, externally influenced read/network/browser/memory-read classes, actor/source separation, approval handling, operator-policy denial, custom-policy weakening guards, concrete tool-id capability mapping, and inert audit-event object creation.

What was not tested: Runtime hook wiring, runtime diagnostic event emission, runtime enforcement, config modes, CLI display, plugin UI, and live tool execution were not tested because this PR introduces only the inert model and audit-event object builder.

## AI assistance

This PR was AI-assisted. I reviewed the generated code and understand the model, evaluator, tests, and documentation.
